description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

As of February 2026 (Reiwa 8), the city has held 9 Emergency Financial Measures Headquarters meetings and 4 Project Team meetings to advance discussions toward sustainable fiscal and administrative operations.

【Goals of the Emergency Financial Measures Plan】
• Secure a combined balance of the Financial Adjustment Fund and Debt Reduction Fund equal to at least 10% of the standard financial scale (1.95 billion yen) by the end of fiscal 2026
• Shift spending to match revenue and identify an appropriate, sustainable budget size

【Actions Taken So Far】
All administrative operations and projects have been reviewed. Administrative Operations Review Sheets have been published in stages. The city is also working on reviewing usage fees and permit charges, studying shorter window service hours, and reducing the mayor's salary and staff personnel costs. A proposed amendment to the District Administrative Center Ordinance in December 2025 (Reiwa 7) was rejected.

【Next Steps】
The city plans to submit the initial budget proposal for fiscal 2026 at the regular city council meeting in March 2026. The entire city administration will work together to achieve the goals by controlling spending and securing revenue.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/sogoseisakubu/zaiseika/kinkyuuzaiseitaisaku/11840.html
summary: ℹ️ Emergency Financial Measures: Status Update (February 2026)
